title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Molly Davis reviews a newspaper regarding the murder of Madame Léon while discussing grocery needs. During this time, Christopher Raine makes unsettling comments to Mrs. Poole about the victim's strangulation, drawing a rebuke from her. The newspaper reports that police are searching for a man of medium height wearing a dark overcoat, a light-colored hat, and a woolen scarf.

At Scotland Yard, Inspector Pembleton interviews two workers, Joe and Bill, regarding the case. They explain that while working on a gas main on Jamman Street, they encountered a man wrapped tightly in clothing who appeared to have a cold. The man gave them a match and fled, accidentally dropping a small notebook. Joe and Bill discovered the notebook contained two addresses—Luxury Farm and 74 Carlisle Street—and the phrase "Three blind mice" written at the top. They noted that shortly after finding the notebook, they heard screams regarding a nearby murder.

Inspector Pembleton shows the notebook to Chief Inspector Kane. They compare it to a piece of paper found pinned to the deceased victim's body, which reads "This is the first one" and features drawings of three mice and sheet music. Kane identifies "Three blind mice" as a signal song. Fingerprint reports confirm the deceased was Morine Gregg, who had been using the alias Madame Lorraine and living at 74 Carlisle Street. The landlady reported that a man claiming to look for the tenant was let into the second floor; ten minutes later, she discovered the tenant had been strangled.
